concept: I fancy a strategy game where players command a fortress built on the back of a giant, slow-moving tortoise that traverses a hazardous landscape. The tortoise's movement cannot be directly controlled but can be influenced by constructing weighted structures on different sections of its shell. Resources must be gathered from the environment as you pass through, requiring quick expeditions while the tortoise briefly pauses in viable harvesting zones. Defensive structures must protect against both environmental hazards and opportunistic attackers drawn to your mobile settlement. The ultimate objective is guiding your tortoise carrier to an ancestral breeding ground where it will birth a new, larger tortoise for your civilization to inhabit.
